sutta: once when the exalted 1 was staying in the brick hall at nadika a householder a good seller visited him saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated the exalted 1 said to him maybe householder arms are given by your family oh yes lord and in this way too such monks as a forest monks arms men that means beggar monks whereas arahants or men 1 to the arahants way to them lord arms are given by my family and the exalted 1 said but surely householder it's a hard thing for you a layman engrossed in pleasures encumbered with children perfumed with car seats sandalwood decked with flowers and scented oils merry with silver and gold to tell which are arahants and which have won through the arahants way if a forest gone monk householder be puffed up proud excitable a multi-specifier forgetful of mouth of mindfulness not self-possessed nor composed a scatter brain uncontrolled in their senses he on that count is blameworthy if he be not puffed up etc but upright in mindfulness self-possessed composed 1 pointed controlled in faculties he on that account is praiseworthy so too of the monk dwelling on the village outskirts the arms men the guest monk the rag wearer the wearer of the rope given by the householder if they be puffed up uncontrolled in their senses on that account they are blameworthy but if on the contrary they are then they are praised worthy nevertheless householder give arms to the sangha as you do so your heart will become tranquil and tranquil in heart you will on the breaking up of the body after death arise in the good way the heaven world and he replied i lord henceforth from today will give arms to the sangha that's the end of the suta
commentary: you see this man he says that his family gives arms food to monks who are arahants who are forest monks who are wearers etc but the buddha said now as a layman it's very hard for you to tell who are arahants and who have won to the arahants way and the buddha said [Music] the most sensible thing to see is whether that monk is unmindful or whether he's mindful whether it is composed or not composed whether his control is in his senses or not control in his senses but nevertheless the buddha said give arms to the sangha as a whole and then you will walk the good way and have a good rebirth now in 1 of the sutas the buddha said it's a duty of laypersons to serve the sangha and in another suta the buddha said in the future there would be those who wear the yellow rope who do not practice well but even then if you give arms to the sangha as a whole you are doing your duty and you help to preserve the sangha that means the lineage of the sangha is not broken and then even though they are not good monks at the moment yet because the sangha is not um broken the the line of of the sangha is not broken then in the future there still can be arahants but if laypersons stop supporting monks because they they are not good practicing monks then the sangha could be broken and then in the future there will be no more monks and the nexuta which is quite a good suitable
